note_data.title: Linear Equations and Inequalities
keyPoints: Linear equation in one variable has the form ax + b = 0, where a ≠ 0
Linear inequality symbols: < (less than), > (greater than), ≤ (less than or equal to), ≥ (greater than or equal to)
Solution of linear equation is the value of variable that satisfies the equation
Graphical representation of linear inequality shows solution set on number line
Properties of inequalities: Adding/subtracting same number preserves inequality direction
Multiplying/dividing by positive number preserves inequality direction
Multiplying/dividing by negative number reverses inequality direction
System of linear equations can be solved by substitution, elimination, or graphical method
Word problems involving linear equations follow: Read → Translate → Solve → Verify pattern

definitions.definition: An equation in which the highest power of the variable is one is called a linear equation.
An inequality in which the highest power of the variable is one is called a linear inequality.
The set of all values of the variable that satisfy the given inequality is called the solution set.
Two or more linear equations in the same variables considered together form a system of linear equations.
A system of equations that has at least one solution is called a consistent system.

importantQuestions.question: Define linear inequality and give an example.
importantQuestions.answer: An inequality in which the highest power of the variable is one is called a linear inequality. Example: 2x + 3 > 7

importantQuestions.question: Solve the system of equations: 2x + 3y = 12 and x - y = 1 by elimination method.
importantQuestions.answer:
Given: 2x + 3y = 12 ... (1) and x - y = 1 ... (2)
From equation (2): x = y + 1 ... (3)
Substituting in equation (1): 2(y + 1) + 3y = 12
2y + 2 + 3y = 12
5y = 10
y = 2
Substituting y = 2 in equation (3): x = 2 + 1 = 3
Therefore, x = 3, y = 2

mcqs.question: Which symbol represents 'greater than or equal to'?

commonMistakes: Students forget to reverse inequality sign when multiplying/dividing by negative numbers
Not checking the solution by substituting back in original equation
In word problems, students fail to define variables clearly before setting up equations
Making arithmetic errors while solving system of equations
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and 1 long question worth 5-8 marks in BSEK papers
Always show step-by-step solution for full marks in subjective questions
Practice drawing number line representations for inequalities
Memorize the exact definitions as they appear in STBB textbook
formulas: ax + b = 0 (Standard form of linear equation)
If a > b and c > 0, then ac > bc
If a > b and c < 0, then ac < bc
note_data.keyDiagram: Number line representation showing solution set of linear inequalities with open/closed circles and arrows indicating direction of inequality
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q3 (4 marks) — asked students to solve a system of linear equations using substitution method and verify the solution
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q7 (6 marks) — required solving linear inequalities and representing solution on number line with proper notation
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q4 (3 marks) — tested definition of linear equation in one variable and identification from given equations
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q8 (5 marks) — word problem involving linear equations with age-related scenario requiring complete algebraic solution

workedExample.question: Solve the linear equation 3(x-2) = 2x + 4 and verify your answer. (4 marks)
steps: Step 5: Verification: LHS = 3(10-2) = 24, RHS = 2(10) + 4 = 24, LHS = RHS ✓
workedExample.answer: The solution is x = 10. Verification confirms this is correct as both sides equal 24.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct expansion, 1 mark for rearranging terms, 1 mark for final answer, 1 mark for verification

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by not providing exact textbook definitions. Always define 'linear equation in one variable' as 'an equation of the form ax + b = 0 where a ≠ 0'
Many students forget to verify their solutions. BSEK specifically awards marks for verification step, so always substitute back into original equation
When solving inequalities, students commonly miss the direction of inequality sign or forget to represent solution on number line with proper open/closed circles
note_data.quickRecap: Linear equation in one variable has form ax + b = 0 where a ≠ 0. Solve by isolating variable through algebraic operations. Linear inequalities use <, >, ≤, ≥ symbols and solutions are represented on number line. Always verify solutions by substitution.
importantFormulas: Standard form of linear equation: ax + b = 0 (where a ≠ 0)
Two-variable linear equation: ax + by + c = 0
For inequality ax + b > 0: x > -b/a (when a > 0)
